revision.text:
[[ja:繁昌神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hanjo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Hanjo shrineNI1407251.JPG |location = {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture |coordinates ={{Coord|35.00024|N|135.757803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} |Rank = |Foundation = {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi Period|1=Engi (era)}} Era (901–922) |Main Building = |Other names = Hanjo no Miya, Kyoto’s Benzaiten, Hannya no Yashiro |Festival = May 20 |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Shimogyō-ku|qid=Q1077022|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}} |deity = {{ill|Ichikishimahime|qid=Q11059454|1=Ichikishima-hime}} }} '''Hanjo Shrine''' (繁昌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Hannya Shrine''' (班女神社), it is colloquially referred to as '''Hanjo no Miya''' (繁昌の宮), '''Kyoto’s Benzaiten''', and '''Hannya no Yashiro''' (班女ノ社). It is the only shrine in Japan specifically dedicated to prosperity in business, as well as blessings for family safety, success in arts, and matchmaking. == Deities == In the early modern period, the shrine enshrined {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}, who is still venerated today.Murakami 2003, p.183 * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime) * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} (Tagirihime) * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} (Tagitsuhime) == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505731|1=Emperor Seiwa}} (858–876), {{ill|Fujiwara no Shigenari|ja|藤原繁成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Shigenari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} had a pond named Kudoku Pond (功徳池) in the garden of his residence. In the {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi Period|1=Engi (era)}}, three deities—{{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}—were transferred from {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} in Aki Province to an island in the pond, establishing Hanjo Shrine. The earliest documented mention of Hanjo Shrine appears in the 13th-century collection {{ill|Uji Shūi Monogatari|en|Uji Shūi Monogatari|qid=Q2446713|lt=Uji Shūi Monogatari|1=Uji Shūi Monogatari}}, which recounts a supernatural tale involving the shrine. By the early {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period|1=Muromachi period}}, it was associated with prosperity in the nearby area. During the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|qid=Q385676|lt=Onin War|1=Ōnin War}}, Hanjo Shrine suffered damage, but it continued to be a focal point of worship. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, Hanjo Shrine was managed by a {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon Sect|1=Shingon Buddhism}} temple called Kudoku-in. However, during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine underwent {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, transitioning fully into a Shinto shrine.[http://www.taishitsu.or.jp/kyoto/sozoro6.html Kyoto Stroll - Hannya Legend] The name "Hannya Shrine" (班女神社) evolved into "Hanjo Shrine" (繁昌神社) over time, possibly influenced by the surrounding area's prosperity. Today, the shrine is located in the Hanjo neighborhood of {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, reflecting its historical significance.Murakami 2003 == location == ;A11ress * 308 Hanjo-chō, Takatsuji-dōri, Muromachi Nishi-iru, Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ;Access * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shijō Station|en|Shijō Station|qid=Q2328455|lt=Shijō Station (Kyoto Municipal Subway)|1=Shijō Station}} on the Kyoto Municipal Subway Karasuma Line. * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Karasuma Station|en|Karasuma Station|qid=Q3969852|lt=Karasuma Station|1=Karasuma Station}} on the Hankyū Kyoto Line. * 3-minute walk from Kyoto City Bus stop "Karasuma Matsubara." == References == * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia,'' Heibonsha, 1997. * Miyako Okada, "The Legends of Hanjo Shrine," ''Setsuwa Bungaku Kenkyū,'' Vol. 35, 2000. * Motomasa Tokue, "Hannya Study," ''Essays on Muromachi Arts,'' Sanmitsu Press, 1984. * Norio Murakami, "Study on Hanjo Shrine," ''East Asian Studies on Supernatural Phenomena,'' Rinshōsha, 2003. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Hanjo Shrine}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|繁昌神社|version=72221097|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:繁昌神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18338914}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Shimogyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
